data.body:
BRAINSTORM /joe-brainstorm — running decision log (1/N).

GROUNDING (3 research streams, code + prior art):
- Code today is REVIEWER-CENTRIC: a workflow agent step's prompt: is resolved via get_reviewer(id) which RAISES if not in reviewers/catalog.json. 'Reviewer' (distinguished by its 'dimension' field) is the only first-class prompt concept; no resolver path exists for a packaged non-reviewer prompt. No input/output contracts exist on prompts (only front-matter variables/required) or scripted ops (docstrings only). Adding mcp_call as a leaf step is structurally cheap (same shape as scripted: with-in, named-outputs-out).
- PRIOR ART: surveyed BAML/DSPy/Pydantic-AI/Humanloop/LangChain Hub/PromptLayer/promptfoo/MS Prompt flow (prompts) + ComfyUI/Rivet/Flowise/LangFlow/n8n/Windmill/Dagster/Prefect (step palettes) + Dify/Rivet/LangFlow (MCP-as-node). Findings: (a) the proposed category taxonomy (review/verify/transform/code/explore) is shipped by NOBODY — novel, so do not make it the load-bearing primitive; (b) DSPy's signature/module split (typed I/O contract orthogonal to single-turn-vs-agentic execution) is the strongest prior art; (c) visual editors validate wiring by NAMED PORT-TYPE matching + an any/* wildcard, NOT structural JSON-Schema checks at connect time (nobody does that); (d) JSON Schema is the lingua franca for INPUT contracts (Windmill, MCP inputSchema); (e) MCP outputSchema is optional & usually absent — every consumer treats MCP output as opaque/any, so we must default to any.

Q1 DECISION — Prompt model primitive: DECOMPOSED TRIPLE (DSPy-style). A prompt declares input_contract + output_contract + execution_mode (single_turn|agentic) EXPLICITLY; 'category' (review/verifier/transform/code/exploration) is an ADVISORY label used only to group the editor palette. Reviewers are a SUBSET = prompts whose output_contract is the findings schema. Editor validates on the actual contracts, not the category. This resolves item 1's 'reviewers are a subset of prompts' and matches the epic's two separate bullets (specify I/O contract per prompt; distinguish single-turn vs agentic).
